title: Indian Railways Officer (IRTS)
description: An Indian Railways Traffic Service (IRTS) officer is responsible for managing the operations and commercial aspects of the Indian Railways. This includes planning, scheduling, and executing train movements, as well as managing freight and passenger services. They play a crucial role in ensuring the efficient and safe transportation of goods and people across the country.
content: IRTS officers are selected through the Civil Services Examination conducted by the UPSC. They undergo rigorous training at the National Academy of Indian Railways (NAIR) in Vadodara. The job involves managing complex logistical operations, coordinating with various departments, and implementing policies to improve efficiency and customer satisfaction. It requires strong leadership, problem-solving, and communication skills.

financialFeasibility.note: While the UPSC exam is accessible to all, coaching and resources can be expensive. Candidates from diverse backgrounds can succeed with dedication and access to quality preparation materials.

sectorScore.remarks: This is primarily a government sector job within the Indian Railways. Private sector opportunities are limited to consultancy roles related to railway infrastructure and logistics.

relatedCareers.reason: Similar administrative and leadership roles within the government sector.
relatedCareers.title: Ias Officer

relatedCareers.reason: Involves managing large-scale operations and maintaining law and order, similar to managing railway operations.
relatedCareers.title: Ips Officer

relatedCareers.reason: Shares skills in supply chain management and transportation planning.
relatedCareers.title: Logistics Manager

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, there is increasing representation of women in the Indian Railways and civil services.
